# Story 15.2: Auto-Generated API Reference & CI Workflow

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a developer browsing the public API,
I want auto-generated API documentation for all public types,
so that I can look up type signatures, method parameters, and XML doc comments.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the source code has XML documentation comments on public types **When** this story is complete **Then** `docs/reference/api/` contains auto-generated API documentation produced by DefaultDocumentation
2. **And** a curated `docs/reference/api/index.md` provides navigable entry points (not just a raw file tree)
3. **And** `.github/workflows/docs-api-reference.yml` exists and triggers on release tags only
4. **And** the workflow builds the solution with DefaultDocumentation, commits generated files to `docs/reference/api/`, and creates a PR with the changes
5. **And** the generated docs render correctly on GitHub
6. **And** NuGet package descriptions contain at least 3 keywords from the SEO keyword list (NFR28: event sourcing, .NET, DAPR, distributed, multi-tenant, event store, CQRS, DDD)

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Enable XML documentation generation (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` to `Directory.Build.props` for packable projects
  - [ ] 1.2 Add `<NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591</NoWarn>` scoped to `Condition="'$(GenerateDocumentationFile)' == 'true'"` so normal dev builds still warn but doc generation doesn't break `TreatWarningsAsErrors`
  - [ ] 1.3 Verify `dotnet build Hexalith.EventStore.slnx --configuration Release` still passes with zero errors
- [ ] Task 2: Install and configure DefaultDocumentation (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1 Add DefaultDocumentation as a **dotnet local tool** via `.config/dotnet-tools.json` (preferred over package reference — simpler for CI, no project coupling)
  - [ ] 2.2 Configure DefaultDocumentation output to `docs/reference/api/` with markdown output format
  - [ ] 2.3 Target the 5 published assemblies: Contracts, Client, Server, Testing, Aspire
  - [ ] 2.4 Run locally and verify markdown output generates correctly for all public types
- [ ] Task 3: Create curated `docs/reference/api/index.md` (AC: #2)
  - [ ] 3.1 Write navigable entry page organized by **namespace-grouped type index** (complements `nuget-packages.md` which already covers package-level narrative — do NOT duplicate that)
  - [ ] 3.2 Include brief description of each namespace's purpose and key types
  - [ ] 3.3 Link to generated sub-pages per namespace/type
  - [ ] 3.4 Add cross-reference to `docs/reference/nuget-packages.md` for package-level context ("For package installation and dependency guidance, see...")
  - [ ] 3.5 Follow page template: back-link, H1, summary paragraph, prerequisites callout, content, Next Steps
- [ ] Task 4: Create `.github/workflows/docs-api-reference.yml` (AC: #3, #4)
  - [ ] 4.1 Trigger on push of `v*` tags only (release tags)
  - [ ] 4.2 Job: checkout, setup .NET 10, restore, build with DefaultDocumentation enabled
  - [ ] 4.3 Commit generated `docs/reference/api/` files to a new branch
  - [ ] 4.4 Create PR with generated changes using `gh pr create` or `peter-evans/create-pull-request` action
  - [ ] 4.5 Add `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by DefaultDocumentation. Do not edit manually. -->` header injection to all generated files
  - [ ] 4.6 Ensure workflow runs on `ubuntu-latest` and completes within CI budget (~45s)
- [ ] Task 5: Verify GitHub rendering (AC: #5)
  - [ ] 5.1 Verify generated markdown renders correctly on GitHub (headings, code blocks, links)
  - [ ] 5.2 Verify cross-links between generated pages resolve correctly
  - [ ] 5.3 Verify curated index.md links to generated pages correctly
- [ ] Task 6: Update NuGet package descriptions for SEO (AC: #6)
  - [ ] 6.1 Review current `Description` in `Directory.Build.props`: "DAPR-native event sourcing server for .NET" — already has 3 keywords (event sourcing, .NET, DAPR) meeting NFR28 minimum
  - [ ] 6.2 Consider adding per-package `<Description>` overrides in individual `.csproj` files for more specific SEO targeting (e.g., Contracts: mention CQRS, DDD; Client: mention multi-tenant; Server: mention distributed)
  - [ ] 6.3 Verify updated descriptions still pass NuGet pack validation
- [ ] Task 7: Update FR traceability and cross-references (AC: #1-6)
  - [ ] 7.1 Update `docs/fr-traceability.md` — set FR19 from `GAP` to `COVERED` referencing `docs/reference/api/index.md`
  - [ ] 7.2 Update gap summary counts in fr-traceability.md
  - [ ] 7.3 Add cross-link from `docs/reference/nuget-packages.md` to `docs/reference/api/index.md`
  - [ ] 7.4 Add entry in README.md docs section if applicable
- [ ] Task 8: Validate with markdownlint-cli2 and verify all internal links resolve

## Dev Notes

### Architecture Decision: DefaultDocumentation

The architecture document (DGAP-2 resolution) selected **DefaultDocumentation** as the API reference generation tool:

| Tool | Score | Verdict |
|------|-------|---------|
| DefaultDocumentation | 4.35/5 | Best auto-gen option |
| xmldoc2md | 3.75/5 | Good alternative |
| DocFX v2 | 2.55/5 | Overpowered, community-maintained |
| Hand-written | 3.40/5 | Adequate for MVP, scales poorly |

**Key constraint:** DefaultDocumentation generates unnavigable file trees on GitHub. The curated `docs/reference/api/index.md` overlay is MANDATORY to address this (AC #2).

### CI Workflow Design (from architecture D3)

```
Phase 2 — docs-api-reference.yml (triggers on release tag only):

Jobs:
  generate-api-docs (ubuntu-latest)
    dotnet build with DefaultDocumentation
    Commit generated docs/reference/api/ files
    Create PR with generated changes
```

**CI budget:** ~45s for API docs generation. Total CI budget (Phase 1a + Phase 2) stays well under NFR23's 5-minute limit.

### 5 Published NuGet Packages to Document

1. `Hexalith.EventStore.Contracts` — Domain types: commands, events, results, identities
2. `Hexalith.EventStore.Client` — Client abstractions and DI registration
3. `Hexalith.EventStore.Server` — Server-side domain processors, DAPR integration
4. `Hexalith.EventStore.Testing` — Testing utilities and helpers
5. `Hexalith.EventStore.Aspire` — .NET Aspire hosting extensions

### NuGet SEO Keyword List (NFR28)

Keywords to include (at least 3 per package description): event sourcing, .NET, DAPR, distributed, multi-tenant, event store, CQRS, DDD

**Current state:**
- `Description` in `Directory.Build.props`: "DAPR-native event sourcing server for .NET" (3 keywords: event sourcing, .NET, DAPR) ✓ meets minimum
- `PackageTags`: `eventsourcing;dapr;cqrs;eventstore;dotnet` (tags are good, descriptions could be more specific per package)

### XML Documentation Comment State

Currently `GenerateDocumentationFile` is NOT enabled in `Directory.Build.props`. Must be added. Be cautious with `TreatWarningsAsErrors` — if XML doc warnings fire for non-documented members, the build will break.

**Resolved decision (party-mode review):** Use `<NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591</NoWarn>` scoped to `Condition="'$(GenerateDocumentationFile)' == 'true'"`. This keeps the warning active in normal dev builds (catching missing docs early) while preventing build failure during doc generation. Scope `GenerateDocumentationFile` to packable projects only.

### File Structure

```
docs/
  reference/
    api/                              # NEW — auto-generated by DefaultDocumentation
      index.md                        # NEW — curated navigable entry page (hand-written)
      Hexalith.EventStore.Contracts/  # Generated
      Hexalith.EventStore.Client/     # Generated
      Hexalith.EventStore.Server/     # Generated
      Hexalith.EventStore.Testing/    # Generated
      Hexalith.EventStore.Aspire/     # Generated
.github/
  workflows/
    docs-api-reference.yml            # NEW — release tag trigger
```

### Generated File Ownership (Party-Mode Decision)

**Do NOT `.gitignore`** `docs/reference/api/` — generated markdown files MUST be committed to the repo for GitHub rendering (AC #5). However, generated files should ONLY be committed via the CI workflow's PR, never manually by developers.

**Guardrails:**
- All generated files (excluding hand-written `index.md`) get an `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by DefaultDocumentation. Do not edit manually. -->` header
- The CI workflow (`docs-api-reference.yml`) is the sole author of generated content
- `index.md` is hand-maintained and tracked normally in git

### Project Structure Notes

- Alignment: `docs/reference/api/` matches architecture D1 folder structure exactly
- Generated files committed to repo (GitHub rendering requirement) — not served via external site
- The curated `index.md` is hand-written and maintained separately from generated content
- `.lycheeignore` may need updating if generated docs contain external links that flake

### Previous Story Intelligence (from Story 15-1)

- **Page template:** back-link `[<- Back to Hexalith.EventStore](../../README.md)`, H1, intro paragraph, prerequisites blockquote, content sections, Next Steps footer
- **markdownlint-cli2** must pass with project config (`.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`)
- **Branch pattern:** `docs/story-15-2-auto-generated-api-reference`
- **Commit pattern:** `feat(docs): Add auto-generated API reference and CI workflow (Story 15-2)`
- **Internal links:** All internal links must resolve to existing files
- **Cross-reference updates** are part of the story (update related docs' Next Steps)
- **Code blocks** need language hints for syntax highlighting
- **FR traceability** update is required (FR19: GAP -> COVERED)

### Git Intelligence

Recent commits show the project is actively maintained with documentation stories following a consistent pattern:
```
4bdc622 Merge pull request #90 from Hexalith/docs/epic-17-sprint-status
68dc11a docs: add Epic 17 (Actor-Based Authorization & Query API) to sprint status
57a4a07 Merge pull request #89 from Hexalith/fix/sdk-version-10.0.103
0790586 fix: bump .NET SDK pin from 10.0.102 to 10.0.103
f06fba3 feat: Enhance command processing with result payload support
```

All doc stories: feature branch per story, single commit with `feat(docs):` prefix, merge via PR.

### DefaultDocumentation Technical Notes

- **Installation:** dotnet local tool via `.config/dotnet-tools.json` (resolved decision — simpler for CI, no project coupling)
- **Output format:** Markdown (`.md` files per type/namespace)
- **Input:** XML documentation files generated by `dotnet build`
- **Key config options:** `--output`, `--assembly`, `--file-name-mode`, `--nested-type-visibility`
- **GitHub rendering:** Generates standard markdown with code fences and links — renders natively on GitHub
- **Limitation:** Flat file tree by default — curated index.md is essential for navigability
